outbound_official.notes: Official Norwegian Ministry of Foreign Affairs (Utenriksdepartementet) document titled 'Norske borgere er visumfrie til følgende land og områder' (Norwegian citizens are visa-free to the following countries and areas). Page confirmed via multiple search results but content could not be scraped directly (HTTP 403). The MFA notes the list may not always be fully current and recommends travelers confirm with the relevant embassy. Supplementary per-country entry details sourced from regjeringen.no/no/tema/utenrikssaker/reiseinformasjon/velg-land/ pages. Schengen/EEA area entries confirmed as free movement rights. USA requires ESTA (electronic travel authorisation, not a consular visa). UK requires UK ETA from 2024. China introduced 30-day visa-free for Norwegian citizens. Namibia moved to visa-on-arrival with a fee from April 2025.

visa_free.name: Schengen Area (all 27 Schengen member/associate states: Austria, Belgium, Czech Republic, Denmark, Estonia, Finland, France, Germany, Greece, Hungary, Iceland, Italy, Latvia, Liechtenstein, Lithuania, Luxembourg, Malta, Netherlands, Poland, Portugal, Slovakia, Slovenia, Spain, Sweden, Switzerland)
EEA member states (freedom of movement: all EU states plus Iceland, Liechtenstein)

visa_on_arrival.name: Namibia
visa_on_arrival.notes: Visa on arrival, fee of NAD 1,600 introduced from April 1, 2025. Previously free. Source: regjeringen.no reiseinfo_namibia.

eta.name: United States
eta.notes: ESTA (Electronic System for Travel Authorization) required before travel. Up to 90 days. Not a consular visa.

eta.name: United Kingdom
eta.notes: UK ETA (Electronic Travel Authorisation) required before travel. Up to 6 months. Introduced 2024. Not a consular visa.

eta.name: Australia
eta.notes: ETA or eVisitor (subclass 601/651) required. Not a consular visa — issued electronically.

eta.name: Canada
eta.notes: eTA (Electronic Travel Authorization) required for air travel. Not a consular visa.

eta.name: Sri Lanka
eta.notes: Electronic Travel Authorization (ETA) required online before arrival.

name: Norway

visa_free.nationality: EU/EEA member states (Austria, Belgium, Bulgaria, Croatia, Cyprus, Czech Republic, Denmark, Estonia, Finland, France, Germany, Greece, Hungary, Ireland, Italy, Latvia, Lithuania, Luxembourg, Malta, Netherlands, Poland, Portugal, Romania, Slovakia, Slovenia, Spain, Sweden, Iceland, Liechtenstein)
visa_free.notes: All EU and EEA citizens enjoy freedom of movement. EEA citizens (including EU nationals) do not require a visa to enter Norway. As EEA members they have the right of residence. Duration subject to EU/EEA freedom of movement rules rather than the 90-day tourist limit. Bulgaria and Romania joined the Schengen area on 22 March 2024.
British Overseas Territory. Bermuda (BDTC) passport holders.

Biometric passport required. The Facilitation Agreement between Norway and Georgia has been partially suspended as of August 15, 2025. Georgian citizens should verify current entry requirements at udi.no before travel.
Schengen associate state (not EU/EEA). Swiss nationals travel visa-free but are subject to the 90-day Schengen limit when visiting as tourists.
Taiwan passport holders with ID card number on the passport.
Post-Brexit, UK nationals including British nationals overseas, overseas territories citizens, overseas citizens, protected persons and subjects are visa-free but subject to 90-day Schengen limit.

Visa-free status confirmed as of 2026. Note: UDI published a January 2026 page regarding Venezuelan nationals unable to depart Norway due to flight cancellations; this does not constitute a suspension of visa-free access.

eta.system: ETIAS (European Travel Information and Authorisation System)
eta.notes: ETIAS will be required for all visa-exempt non-EU/EEA/Swiss nationals (including UK, US, Canada, Australia and all other current visa-free nationalities except EU/EEA/Swiss) to travel to Norway and the full Schengen area. Norway is one of the 30 Schengen member and associated states participating. As of June 2026, ETIAS is not yet in force; existing visa-free arrangements remain valid until the system launches.

visa_policy.notes: Norway is a Schengen member state. All EU/EEA nationals enjoy freedom of movement rights (no visa required, no 90-day limit as tourists). Non-EU/EEA visa-free visitors may stay up to 90 days within any 180-day period in the Schengen area. No national e-visa system found on official UDI site. Some countries require biometric passports. Georgia exemption partially suspended as of August 15, 2025. Switzerland is a Schengen associate (not EU/EEA) and travels visa-free subject to the 90-day limit. ETIAS scheduled for Q4 2026 launch — once active, all non-EU/EEA/Swiss visa-exempt nationals will need ETIAS pre-travel authorisation. Skilled worker permits now require employer confirmation codes (Feb 2026 update).

credential.subtype: any valid residence permit in a Schengen country

conditional_access.conditions: Persons with a valid residence permit in a Schengen country may enter Norway visa-free for up to 90 days. Must bring their residence card and passport.

credential.subtype: residence card in Ireland or Cyprus issued on basis of EU/EEA family member

conditional_access.conditions: Persons who have a residence card in a Schengen country, Ireland or Cyprus because they have a family member who is a citizen of another EU/EEA country may enter Norway visa-free for up to 90 days. Must bring their residence card and passport.

School pupils who are third-country nationals on an organized school trip within the EU may enter Norway visa-free if they are included on a group travel document issued by an EU member state and accompanied by a teacher. Based on Schengen acquis.
Seafarers with authorized identity documents recognized under international maritime conventions may be exempt from visa requirements when their vessel calls at a Norwegian port.
UN civil servants holding a valid UN Laissez-Passer are exempt from visa requirements for Norway.
Refugees holding a travel document issued by Malta, Ireland, Liechtenstein, or Switzerland are exempt from visa requirements for Norway.
Albanian diplomatic passports — applies to accredited ambassadors to Norway and their spouses and children only.
Armenian diplomatic passports — applies to diplomats who are not going to work at the embassy in Norway.
Azerbaijani diplomatic passport — applies to diplomats who are not going to work at the embassy in Norway.
Bolivian diplomatic and service passports.
Chinese diplomatic passport — applies to diplomats who are not going to work at the embassy in Norway.
Indian diplomatic passports — applies to diplomats who are not going to work at the embassy in Norway.
Indonesian diplomatic and service passports — applies to diplomats who are not going to work at the embassy in Norway.
Jordanian diplomatic passports — applies to diplomats who are not going to work at the embassy in Norway.
Kazakhstani diplomatic passports — applies to diplomats who are not going to work at the embassy in Norway.
Laotian diplomatic passports — applies to diplomats who are not going to work at the embassy in Norway.
North Macedonian diplomatic and service passports.
Moldovan diplomatic passports.
Moroccan diplomatic, service and special passports.
Pakistani diplomatic passports and official passports.
Philippine diplomatic, service and special passports.
South African diplomatic and official passports.
Thai diplomatic and official passports.
Tunisian diplomatic passports.
Turkish diplomatic, service and special passports.
Ukrainian diplomatic passports.
United Arab Emirates diplomatic and special passports.

cbi.notes: Norway does not offer a Citizenship-by-Investment (CBI) program. Norwegian citizenship is obtained through naturalization based on genuine residence. Dual citizenship is explicitly permitted (confirmed on UDI citizenship page: 'you do not need to notify UDI that you want to keep your other citizenship'). A December 2025 update references 'sufficient income for reduced residence time when applying for citizenship' but specific thresholds are not published on the main page. Standard naturalization path requires years of qualifying residence; exact duration varies by applicant profile. No passive investment route to citizenship exists. Source: https://www.udi.no/en/want-to-apply/citizenship/

programs.name: Residence Permit for Self-Employed / Sole Proprietor (Non-EU/EEA)
programs.notes: Non-EU/EEA nationals with completed higher education or vocational training may apply for a residence permit as a self-employed sole proprietor in Norway. The business must be a sole proprietorship (not a limited company), must generate profit of at least NOK 341,373 per year pre-tax (confirmed on official page as of 2026). Applicant must actively participate in the business. Permanent residence is available after 3 years of eligible residence. This is not a passive investment program — active business participation is required. Citizenship timeline not stated on RBI page; citizenship general requirements vary by profile.

programs.name: Skilled Worker Residence Permit
programs.eligibility: Non-EU/EEA nationals with completed vocational training (3+ years upper secondary) or university/college degree, or special qualifications through 6+ years professional experience. Must have a concrete full-time (or at least 80%) job offer from a Norwegian employer. Pay and conditions must meet Norwegian standards.
programs.processing_time: No official fast-track scheme. Processing may be extended for vocational qualifications from Bangladesh, China, India, Iran, Kosovo, Nepal, Pakistan, Türkiye, and Vietnam. As of February 2026, applicants must obtain an employer confirmation code before submitting applications (fraud-prevention measure).
programs.notes: Permits for vocational-level positions granted up to 1 year; for university-level positions up to 3 years. No annual quotas or caps. Path to permanent residence in 3 years. If job is lost, holder may remain up to 6 months to seek new employment. Minimum income threshold is NOK 341,373 per year pre-tax.

programs.name: Job Seeker Residence Permit
programs.eligibility: Skilled workers who recently graduated from Norwegian education or are researchers. Must have sufficient financial support of at least NOK 28,448 per month. Must have higher education degree or vocational training.
programs.processing_time: Not specified on official page; see UDI waiting times page.
programs.notes: Duration: maximum 1 year. Period does NOT count toward permanent residence. Cannot be self-employed. Can work full or part-time in any job while seeking skilled employment. If job offer received during processing, application can be converted to skilled worker status at no extra cost.

agreements: EEA (European Economic Area) freedom of movement

validation_notes: CHANGED: Added ETIAS forthcoming ETA entry in visa_policy.eta — ETIAS launches Q4 2026 for all non-EU/EEA/Swiss visa-exempt nationals to Norway; previously missing from record.

CHANGED: Switzerland notes corrected — previously noted only as 'Schengen member state' but Switzerland is a Schengen ASSOCIATE, not an EU/EEA member; subject to 90-day Schengen tourist limit unlike EEA citizens.
CHANGED: Taiwan notes updated — clarified that Taiwan passport holders must have their ID card number on the passport (UDI condition confirmed on official page).

CHANGED: United Kingdom notes updated — expanded to include British nationals overseas, overseas territories citizens, overseas citizens, protected persons and subjects as covered by the visa-free arrangement.
CHANGED: Bermuda notes updated — added BDTC passport type clarification.
CHANGED: Venezuela notes updated — added clarification that January 2026 UDI notice was about Venezuelan nationals stranded in Norway due to cancelled flights, NOT a suspension of visa-free access.

CHANGED: Added 4 new conditional_access entries previously missing: school pupils on organized EU trips, seafarers with authorized identity documents, UN Laissez-Passer holders, and refugees with travel documents from Malta/Ireland/Liechtenstein/Switzerland.

CHANGED: rbi.path_to_citizenship_years changed from 8 to null — UDI citizenship page confirms requirements vary by applicant profile; citing 8 years was an oversimplification not directly sourced from UDI page.
CHANGED: fast_track skilled worker notes updated — added February 2026 employer confirmation code requirement.
CHANGED: cbi.notes updated to reflect dual citizenship confirmation directly scraped from UDI page (not general knowledge).
CHANGED: EU/EEA bloc notes updated to note Bulgaria and Romania joined Schengen on 22 March 2024.
CONFIRMED: Georgia partial suspension of Facilitation Agreement (15 August 2025) verified on official UDI page.
CONFIRMED: Self-employed minimum NOK 341,373 per year threshold verified on official UDI page.
gaps: ETIAS exact launch date not confirmed by UDI directly; Q4 2026 timeline from EU/European Commission sources (eeas.europa.eu). UDI has not published an ETIAS-specific page yet.

Citizenship residency duration (years) not directly extracted from UDI page — page states requirements vary by applicant profile and links to sub-pages. '8 of 11 years' is a known general rule but not confirmed by direct scraping in this session.
December 2025 income sufficiency update for reduced citizenship residence time — threshold not published on main UDI citizenship page; sub-page or application form may contain figures.
Barents Region border residents (mentioned on UDI as a special access category) — full conditions and eligible nationalities not extracted in detail.
Diplomatic/service passport waiver exact treaty dates and treaty names not published on the UDI list page.
Georgia facilitation agreement partial suspension — exact scope of what is suspended vs. retained not detailed on the main UDI page; Georgian citizens should verify directly.
Aviation personnel with valid licenses mentioned on UDI as a special access category — conditions not extracted in full.
Specific processing times for skilled worker and job seeker permits not stated on official pages; UDI refers to a separate processing times page.
